> **Windows Defender / SmartScreen warning:** The Windows installer is not Authenticode-signed yet, so Microsoft Defender or your browser may call it an unknown app, suspicious download, or generic trojan even when it was downloaded by the app updater. To verify it, upload the exact file to [VirusTotal](https://www.virustotal.com) and check that any detections are generic heuristic labels, not a named malware family. To install after SmartScreen blocks it, click **More info**, then **Run anyway**; if your browser blocks the download, choose **Keep**. If Defender quarantines the file, report it as a false positive to Microsoft from **Windows Security → Protection history** and allow-list the installer or install folder only after you verify the release.
